body: Something wrong with our results, they are too verbose, we should show only short quote from the found results like google does, we should normalize all results from all data sources. Also my requirements from https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/153 was ignored on how to style the results. We should show url + title, for each item (at lease the domain), and also fragment of the text (a quote containing the original request) and `Read more`. ``` WP:Яблоко (партия) [Яблоко (партия)](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%AF%D0%B1%D0%BB%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%BE_(%D0%BF%D0%B0%D1%80%D1%82%D0%B8%D1%8F)) — через wikipedia#2 - общественное объединение «ЯБЛОКО», а 15 марта 1998 года как общероссийская политическая общественная организация «Объединение ЯБЛОКО». 26 апреля 2002 года Q15332 [Yabloko](http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q15332) — через wikidata#2 - Russian political party ``` Duplicate entities are still not joined. And in case of duplication we should add last separate line like (with no details, just other sources): ``` Другие источники: [Викидата](http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q15332) ``` Priority is like this: DuckDuckGo, Internet Archive (archive.org), Wikipedia, Wikidata, Wiktionary and after that other resources, which are available. We can try to make requests to them all once, to understand if they available or not, and remember that until browser window will be closed (so it will be in ram, just to reduce number of broken requests). So if we get CORS, we don't do requests in that time. button for menu collapse/expand and source code button does not support dark theme. Also menu to the left, should be always in single column on mobile and on desktop. And it should be collapsable, so it will be enough space for conversations list and other sections. Also these badges are still does not fit the content, and markup is still broken in diagnostics mode: Also dark theme not supported in new UI: And in each steps, I don't see raw requests and responses. We need to deeply rethink that, as I clearly asked many times we should show each reasoning step, its inputs and outputs, if it has associated http requests, I want to see full raw requests and responses (expandable of course), how they were converted to unified links notation for that type of data and so on. I want to see everything to understand what exactly is going on. Please make it as user friendly as possible in diagnostics mode, users will see it, and also want to understand that. Please make it feel real, not something fake. A real traceable reasoning steps, action steps and so on. We need to have the best possible architecture for that. Also I multiple times asked to do actual reasoning instead of memoization. In all cases we should start by formalization of the text/message, then reason up on formalized version, and after reasoning complete and all actions executed we should reason how to present the answer, and deformalize it (convert to actual natural language). Double check we use best possible architecture so it will be easy to grow up on. We need twice as many unit, integration and e2e tests for all cases, we should deeply test each reasoning step and so on. We should test UI components and so on. Everything should be 100% covered with tests and done fully in deepest and widest possible way. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
